title: toxicodendron striatum
intro: Toxicodendron striatum, commonly known as Poison Sumac, is a plant species that belongs to the Anacardiaceae family. This plant is rich in health benefits, including being used to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The therapeutic actions of Poison Sumac include its ability to lower fever, reduce pain and swelling, and exhibit antioxidant activities. The bioactive constituents of this plant include alkaloids, terpenoids, and flavonoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
side_effects: Toxicodendron striatum can have side effects if used improperly, such as severe skin irritation, blistering, and rashes due to its toxic sap, which can cause an allergic reaction in some individuals. Prolonged exposure to the plant's toxic compounds can also lead to systemic symptoms like fever, headache, and nausea. In extreme cases, improper use of the plant can cause anaphylaxis, a life-threatening allergic reaction that requires immediate medical attention. If ingested, the plant's toxic compounds can cause gastrointestinal issues, including vomiting and abdominal pain.
